rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM VEDERMAN M.D VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Administrative Writ, Writ Of Mandate, And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER WILLIAM VEDERMAN'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Administrative Writ, Writ Of Mandate, And Attorneys Fees. Petitioner William Vederman's petition for writ of mandate is denied. Respondent Medical Board provided a hearing to Dr. Vederman to enable him to contest the Board's position that he violated the terms of his probation by failing to engage in "activities defined in sections 2051 and 2052 of the Business and Professions Code." Dr. Vederman fails to show that the Board's findings that he did not participate in activities encompassed with sections 2051 and 2052 were contrary to the weight of the evidence. The testimony of Dr. Lockmiller amply supported the Board's conclusion that Dr. Vederman's continued practice of administrative medicine did not meet the requirements of his probation. Notably Dr. Vederman did not persuasively identify any activities that he engaged in that are covered by section 2051 or section 2052. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Board is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Leave To File Against City And County Of San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER PATRINA HALL'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Leave To File Against City And County Of San Francisco. Petitioner Patrina Hall's petition for an order relieving her from her failure to present a timely Government Code claim to respondent City and County of San Francisco is granted. Ms. Hall complied with all of the requirements of Government Code 946.6(b) and has shown that her application to the City to present a late claim was made within a reasonable time and that her failure to present a timely claim to the City was due to mistake and excusable neglect. Per Government Code 946.6(f), petitioner may file a complaint within thirty days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Hall is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO CRUZ ET AL VS. PROFESSIONAL CHARTER SERVICES LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS PROFESSIONAL CHARTER SERVICES LLC AND BRENDA GUTIERREZ' MOTION TO TRANSFER. Defendants Professional Charter Services, LLC and Brenda Gutierrez's motion to change venue per CCP 397(c) is denied. Longstanding case law cited in the opposing memorandum establishes that a section 397(c) motion must be filed within a reasonable time after the case is at issue. At this stage of the consolidated cases, when more than twenty months have elapsed since defendants filed an answer to the complaint in the first filed action and less than six weeks to trial, it would be extremely unfair and prejudicial to plaintiffs for these cases to be transferred to another county. Therefore, defendants' failure to file this motion within a reasonable time after answering the complaint in the first filed action is sufficient basis to deny the motion. Even if the motion was timely filed, it would be denied since defendants have not shown that convenience of the witnesses and the ends of justice support the transfer of this case to Tulare County. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff Michelle Vera is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. JOHN GUEST USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant John Guest Usa Inc. Continued Hearing On Motion For Issue Or Evidentiary Sanctions (Specially Set Per Jpt N. Lebowitz)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 1, Defendant John Guest Usa Inc.'s Continued Hearing On Motion For Issue Or Evidentiary Sanctions (Specially Set Per Jpt N. Lebowitz) Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant John Guest Usa Inc.'s Continued Hearing On Motion For Issue Or Evidentiary Sanctions is continued to July 25, 2018 at 1:30 p.m. per the agreement of the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY HOUGH ET AL VS. TODD COURAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories To Mark Hamilton, Lynn Schwarzhoff, And Christopher Scorfina, Set One, Number 15.1; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Mark Hamilton, Christipher Scorfina And Lynn Schwarzhoff And They Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $942
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS WENDY HOUGH, MATTHEW HRUDKA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories To Mark Hamilton, Lynn Schwarzhoff, And Christopher Scorfina, Set One, Number 15.1; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Mark Hamilton, Christipher Scorfina And Lynn Schwarzhoff And Their Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $942. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted to the extent it appears a Supplemental Response to Form Interrogatory 15.1 was served after filing the motion to compel. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $960.00 are awarded solely against Defendants' counsel to be paid within twenty days of the date of the Order to be entered herein. The meet and confer exchange of letters between counsel fully supports filing the motion. The Court also finds no justification that the facts now set forth in the Supplemental Response were not known and thereby available to be stated in the initial response. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REGIONAL PROTECTION AGENCY, INC. VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT U.S. SECURITY ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant U.S. Security Associates, Inc.'s motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages in the complaint filed by plaintiff Regional Protection Agency, Inc. is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Regional Protection clarified that it is only seeking punitive damages against US Security on the third cause of action for interference with contractual relations. The third cause of action fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support punitive damages. If it can do so in good faith, Regional Protection is granted leave to allege facts showing that one or more officers, directors, or managing agents of US Security acted with malice, fraud or oppression in interfering with Regional Protection's employment contracts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for US Security is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
